mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production (INDPRO) signals stronger real economic activity, which increases demand for capital and pushes long-term yields higher over the next 20 trading days as investors price in tighter monetary conditions.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes show a correlation of less than 0.05 with 20-day yield changes over any 5-year rolling window, or the signal accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market weakness that prompts expectations of Federal Reserve easing and safe-haven bond buying, driving the 10-year yield lower over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points within 3 months
failure_condition: In at least 60% of trigger events the 20-day yield does not decline, or the mean yield change is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation outpaces the federal funds rate, real interest rates become negative, compelling investors to demand higher nominal yields to compensate for eroded purchasing power, lifting the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: When the trigger condition holds, the subsequent 20-day yield change is positive less than 55% of the time, or the average yield change is not significantly above zero.

mechanism_narrative: Above-trend growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which raises expectations for economic expansion and tighter monetary policy, leading to higher 10-year yields over the next 20 trading days during periods of strong retail sales growth.
failure_condition: When the trigger is active, the 20-day yield change is negative more than 50% of the time, or the average yield change is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) act as a leading indicator of economic strength; a sharp monthly increase signals rising construction activity and broader demand, which pushes 10-year yields upward over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations intensify.
failure_condition: When HOUST rises more than 5%, the 20-day yield change is negative in over half of cases, or the forward yield change has no systematic sign bias.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP (GDPC1) reflects an expanding economy that increases the demand for credit and lifts the natural rate of interest, putting upward pressure on the 10-year yield over the next 60 trading days regardless of short-term noise.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth has a rolling 5-year correlation with 20-day yield changes of less than 0.10, or the directional accuracy is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) and very low unemployment (UNRATE) create a tight labor market that amplifies wage inflation fears and forces bond yields higher as investors anticipate more aggressive monetary tightening over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, 20-day yield changes are negative in more than 40% of instances, or the average yield change is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a keenly watched leading indicator; during a sustained housing boom (defined by elevated starts levels), any positive momentum in starts reinforces growth optimism and pushes the 10-year yield higher over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when HOUST is above its 12-month moving average, the 5-day yield change is negative more than half the time, or the signal fails to have a statistically positive return.
